# I WANT TO START AN AGENCY BUT NEVER DO (here's why & how to fix it)
**Channel:** Adam Erhart
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kjk1Vq5gceU
**Date:** 2026-04-15
**Content source:** auto-generated transcript

## Summary
Adam Erhart identifies why aspiring agency owners get stuck in planning mode and never launch. The core issue isn't lack of knowledge—it's the failure to take concrete action. He explains "the flip": the mindset shift that happens when you do something real (like buying a domain), which changes your self-identity from planner to owner and enables momentum toward getting your first client.

## Key Points & Strategies

- **Three Positions Model**: Planner (consuming content) → The Flip (taking concrete action) → Owner (actively acquiring clients)
- **The Core Problem**: Information consumption creates false progress; planning feels productive but doesn't generate clients
- **Action Over Readiness**: Confidence comes *after* action, not before. Waiting to "feel ready" is procrastination disguised as preparation
- **The Flip Defined**: The single smallest concrete action you can complete today that proves you've started (not the most impressive one)
- **Examples of "The Flip"**:
  - Buy a domain name with your name/agency name
  - Write down your offer in one sentence (what you do + who you do it for)
  - Send one message to one person saying you're starting an agency and looking for your first client
- **Psychological Shift**: Real action creates evidence; evidence changes self-perception; changed self-perception makes the next action easier
- **Momentum Chain**: First action → easier second action → even easier third action
- **Comfort as the Real Barrier**: The planner box isn't comfortable because of information gaps—it's comfortable because it avoids the discomfort of putting your name on something real

## Workflows & Tools Discussed

- **HighLevel**: Mentioned as the software used to build websites quickly (domain + template setup in ~10 minutes)
- **Basic Workflow**: Buy domain → build basic website → reach out to potential clients

## Use Cases Covered

- Starting an AI agency with no paying clients yet
- Starting a marketing agency from scratch
- Moving from solopreneur planning to actual agency owner identity
- First-time cold outreach to potential clients after taking initial action

## Quotes Worth Noting

> "Action is proof, and proof is what actually changes how you see yourself. Not a course or another video, a real action in the real world. That's the flip."

> "Action first, confidence second, every single time."

> "Every master was once a disaster. The confidence that you're waiting for doesn't come from more preparation. It comes from evidence. And the only way to get evidence is to do something real."
